soma.insight_of_week: Founders Pledge $5M recommendation has progressed to philanthropy partner review (Lorcan email July 14). Second limited DD round expected by July 31. Michael must prepare focused responses on SOMA programme redesign (July 2026 board changes: dropped TaRL/WhatsApp bot, locked AI analytics + assessment + dashboard + Mwalimu Kinara) before Lorcan's questions arrive. Decision timeline: Q1-Q2 2027.

hot_list.why_now: Lorcan's July 14 email signals internal FP recommendation made. Partner review phase = highest probability moment before final decision. Second DD questions imminent (by July 31). Window to shape narrative on SOMA redesign is NOW.
hot_list.recommended_action: Prepare 1-page briefing for Lorcan on July 2026 SOMA board redesign: assessment + ability grouping + dashboard + Mwalimu Kinara + AI analytics. Emphasise cost-lock ($3.12/student/year) and LAYS modelling. Pre-empt likely questions on dropped TaRL prescription and WhatsApp coaching bot (not fit-for-purpose). Do not send unsolicited; wait for Lorcan's second DD set, then respond swiftly.

hot_list.why_now: Track 1 ($100K) signed July 23. Bank transfer verified Monday July 27. Ali Ellegard is warm and ready. Immediate follow-up window (next 2 weeks) to initiate Track 2 SOMA conversation while momentum is high.
hot_list.recommended_action: Email Ali Ellegard by July 28: 'Track 1 onboarding underway. We'd like to explore Track 2 for SOMA's AI predictive analytics layer (KiuFunza + Uwezo historical data feeding live learning dashboard). This completes the assessment → insight → coaching improvement cycle. Could we schedule a 30-min call in first week of August to scope fit?' Frame as natural 10-year partnership extension.

hot_list.why_now: VTF KiuFunza agreement counter-signature expected by ~July 30 (Sandra's timeline). Post-signature, SOMA conversation can begin without conflating the asks. VTF's 80% tech mandate is perfectly aligned with AI dashboard + predictive analytics.
hot_list.recommended_action: On or after July 30 (when agreement fully signed), email Sandra Oswald: 'We'd like to explore how SOMA's AI analytics + live learning dashboard could extend VTF's technology impact in KiuFunza schools. Frame as tech platform opportunity, not a separate funding ask. Could we propose a 5-year partnership structure (reflecting VTF's multi-year commitment signal from July 14)?' Deadline to start: first week of August.

hot_list.why_now: Google for Nonprofits ad grant pipeline is IMMEDIATE PRIORITY. Website live → Goodstack submission (10-14 days) → activate $10K/month search credits. Zero-cost infrastructure win to unlock Google.org Impact Challenge relationship-building.
hot_list.recommended_action: URGENT: Confirm learnimpact.org website goes live THIS WEEK (by July 25). Upon live verification, submit Goodstack application immediately. Rolling 10-14 day approval. Parallel: assign someone to prepare Google.org Impact Challenge tracking sheet (monitor for Africa-specific calls, typically 3-6 months advance notice). This establishes Google relationship foundation before seeking $1M+ impact grant.

hot_list.why_now: Gates Foundation just announced $40M AI-education partnership with ADQ for sub-Saharan Africa (Dec 2025). SOMA's AI predictive analytics + KiuFunza RCT evidence + cost-lock ($3.12/student/year) are direct fit to Gates' current priority shift. Prospect not yet warm, but strategic window is NOW.
hot_list.recommended_action: Research Gates Foundation's AI-education sub-Saharan Africa initiative (Dec 2025 announcement + 2025 Global Education Program $240M commitment). Identify programme officer for foundational learning + AI. Prepare 2-page expression of interest positioning SOMA as Gates-funded scale pathway: RCT evidence, cost-effectiveness narrative, government partnership model. Target submission: September 2026.

hot_list.why_now: Hempel Foundation spontaneously signalled renewal for KiuFunza at May 2026 Steering Committee. Hearing Committee decision is LIVE. No explicit ask yet, but this is highest-probability existing funder. Relationship health is 'partner' with 70% probability.
hot_list.recommended_action: Send brief (1-page) KiuFunza impact summary to Hempel by August 1: outcomes from Phase 5, teacher incentive programme feedback, link to SOMA teacher recognition component (Mwalimu Kinara). Signal that SOMA is a complementary system-strengthening layer that amplifies KiuFunza teacher impact. Prep for renewal conversation Q4 2026.

hot_list.why_now: Tanzania GPE grant ($88M, 2026) is active. SOMA can position as NGO delivery complement to government programme. GPE's Global Coalition for Foundational Learning is explicitly aligned with SOMA's 4-component model. Entry requires PMO-RALG door-opener, but window is NOW (year 1 of grant cycle).
hot_list.recommended_action: Schedule call with Michael + Youdi to map PMO-RALG relationship (Ministry of Education contact for GPE). If relationship exists, prepare expression of interest framing SOMA as NGO implementation partner for GPE foundational learning outcomes in 2-3 pilot districts. If no existing PMO-RALG contact, identify via Ministry network by end August. Target LOI submission: October 2026 (Q4 grant cycle window).

priority_shifts.what_changed: Internal recommendation made (Lorcan email July 14). Progressed from initial DD to philanthropy partner review phase. Second limited DD round now expected (by July 31) before final decision Q1-Q2 2027.
priority_shifts.action: Prepare lean, focused responses on SOMA redesign (July 2026 board changes). Do not over-explain. Emphasise cost-lock and LAYS evidence. Anticipate questions on programme simplification (dropped TaRL/WhatsApp bot).

priority_shifts.what_changed: KiuFunza grant agreement now signed by LearnImpact (July 9). Awaiting VTF counter-signature (~July 30). Sandra's July 14 signal: VTF wants 5-year partnership frame (not annual renewals). This is a strategic shift to long-term commitment structure.
priority_shifts.action: Post-signature (by Aug 1), begin SOMA conversation with Sandra framed as 5-year partnership extension. AI analytics + dashboard = tech platform play, not separate funding ask. Aligns with VTF's public commitment signal (documentary team visit to Pangani & Kilindi, June 29).

soma.positioning_advice: Michael should author or contribute to 2-3 pieces in the next 30 days to stay warm with Gates, Founders Pledge, and Google.org: (1) Co-authored short-form piece on 'Cost-Effective AI for Foundational Learning: Evidence from Tanzania KiuFunza RCT' (target: LAYS metric framing; pitch to Gates + FP). (2) Podcast or webinar appearance on 'Learning Dashboards as Government Systems Strengthening Tools' (target: GPE + FCDO audiences; emphasise government co-financing and SOMA Kibaha Mtwara pathway). (3) LinkedIn op-ed: 'Why Programme Redesign Matters: Dropping Complexity, Locking Cost-Effectiveness' (broad positioning of SOMA's July 2026 board decisions as evidence-driven simplification; reach early-stage fundraisers and impact investors like Vadim). Avoid Twaweza history. Lead with QJE evidence, government partnership, and AI as systems tool — not tech for tech's sake. Emphasise 'newly independent' framing (May 2026) as signal of institutional maturity and board governance.

kiufunza.insight_of_week: Hempel Foundation Board visit to Tanzania Sept 27–30 is the single most critical cultivation moment of 2026. Rosa Robinson has signalled renewal is possible but formally LOW probability (extension already given). Anders Holm (HF Director) attended SC for first time May 27 and showed genuine interest in the incentives model. Prepare intensively: brief Michael on HF's business-sector interest in performance pay; stage a compelling school visit narrative; have Youdi ready to present KiuFunza QJE evidence if needed. This visit determines whether HF extends beyond 2026. Failure to activate here risks losing USD 587K anchor donor.

hot_list.why_now: Board visit Sept 27–30 is imminent (66 days). Rosa unprompted mentioned renewal at Oxford April; Anders Holm showed first-time interest at SC May 27. Renewal formally LOW probability but culturally possible if visit is executed well. USD 587K confirmed FY2026 is anchor grant — protect above all.
hot_list.recommended_action: Michael: Schedule pre-visit briefing with Youdi (KiuFunza evidence), Rosa (relationship lead), and LI operations team. Prepare school visit narrative highlighting teacher incentives + business-sector angle (Anders mentioned HF board members interested in incentives). Draft 1-pager on 'KiuFunza Year 2 Impact & Year 3 Roadmap' for Anders/board. Do NOT push for renewal directly — let Rosa lead. Confirm Sept 27 event details + Sept 30 school logistics with VVOB immediately.

hot_list.why_now: Lorcan Clarke emailed July 14 confirming grant recommendation shared with FP's philanthropy partner. USD 5M 4-year SOMA ask under review. Expect second (limited) set of DD questions second half July. Timeline to decision is Q1–Q2 2027 but questions are imminent (within 14 days). Parallel Vadim track (FP member) may unlock personal giving if recommendation is accepted.
hot_list.recommended_action: Youdi + Michael: Prepare for limited DD round expected by July 31. Likely focus: SOMA budget breakdown, AI coaching cost model, government co-financing contingency. hot_list.why_now: Track 1 USD 100K SIGNED July 2026. Bank transfer in process (July 27). Ali Ellegard (Programme Officer) is warm after Track 1 win. Track 2 SOMA conversation NOT YET started. Potential for 10-year partnership if pitched strategically.
hot_list.recommended_action: Michael: After bank transfer confirmation (late July), schedule Track 2 exploratory call with Ali. Frame SOMA Track 2 as natural progression: 'Track 1 gave us the assessment baseline; Track 2 is the AI coaching loop that closes the improvement cycle.' Position as year 2–3 expansion of Dovetail's assessment investment. Potential ask: USD 150–250K over 2 years for AI coaching component.

hot_list.why_now: Arnold Ventures is Tier 1 prospect with 85% alignment (RCT-backed evidence, government integration, cost-effectiveness, scale pathway). Probability currently 38% but effort score is high (4). No recent news — opportunity to differentiate. KiuFunza QJE publication + SOMA government co-financing model are direct fit to AV's core thesis.

Send to Arnold via warm introduction (check if Founders Pledge or HF have AV contacts). Target: exploratory call Q4 2026. Budget: USD 500K 2-year ask for SOMA scaling.

hot_list.why_now: World Bank Education Global Practice just announced $15B Global Coalition for Foundational Learning (2026–2030). LearnImpact's LAYS-based cost-effectiveness model + government systems angle are direct fit. Tanzania education sector is active. Probability currently 30% but effort is high (5).
hot_list.recommended_action: Michael: Identify World Bank contact via Youdi or HF network (Rosa Robinson may have connections). Draft inquiry: 'Is LearnImpact eligible for GCFL funding? Can SOMA model be co-financed with World Bank in-country education budget?' Target: exploratory call late Q3 2026. Do NOT lead with Twaweza history.

hot_list.why_now: William & Flora Hewlett Foundation continues Sub-Saharan Africa foundational learning focus (2025–2026). 82% alignment (deeper learning, evidence-based, government capacity). Probability 35% but effort is high (4). No recent news but SOMA foundational assessment component is direct fit.
hot_list.recommended_action: Michael: Research Hewlett's 2026 education RFP calendar. If no open RFP, prepare 3-page concept note for Q4 2026 inquiry: 'SOMA as Government-Embedded Foundational Learning System.' Target: USD 300–500K seed for Mtwara RCT governance/data dashboard component. Use Youdi or HF network for warm intro.

priority_shifts.what_changed: Sandra Oswald signalled (July 14) that VTF wants current KiuFunza agreement updated to reflect 5-year commitment, not annual renewal cycle. This represents a strategic shift from transactional to relational contracting.
priority_shifts.action: Michael + Ragate: Once VTF agreement is fully counter-signed (~July 30), propose 5-year MoU framework for SOMA. Frame as 'deepening partnership' not 'adding budget.' This unlocks multi-year planning confidence and increases SOMA ask credibility.

priority_shifts.what_changed: Lorcan Clarke's July 14 email signals positive internal recommendation momentum. Phrase 'our partner may accept, adjust, or choose not to make the grant' indicates FP's internal process is moving toward decision gate. Second DD round expected second half July indicates partner is taking it seriously (not routine diligence).
priority_shifts.action: Michael: Prepare for focused, technical second round of questions. High probability these will target SOMA financial model robustness, government co-financing contingency, and disbursement schedule. Have budget detail ready by July 27.

kiufunza.positioning_advice: Michael should author or co-author ONE thought piece in next 30 days targeting Hempel, Founders Pledge, and World Bank audiences. Recommended framing: 'Government-Embedded Incentive Systems as the Bridge Between Evidence and Scale: Lessons from KiuFunza and SOMA.' Publish as: (1) LinkedIn article (tag Rosa Robinson, Lorcan Clarke, Anders Holm); (2) EdData or similar education research platform; or (3) short blog post on LearnImpact website. Key messages: (i) KiuFunza QJE result (9.4pp) proves teacher incentives work at scale in low-resource contexts; (ii) SOMA's government co-financing model (Kibaha District Council) de-risks donor dependency; (iii) AI analytics layer (predictive, not prescriptive) respects teacher autonomy while improving targeting. Avoid any mention of Twaweza. This positions Michael as a pragmatist bridge-builder between rigorous evidence and political feasibility — exactly what HF's Anders Holm signalled interest in at the May SC. Timing: publish by August 31 to warm prospects before Sept (Hempel visit) and Q4 (Arnold, Hewlett exploratory outreach).

timamu.insight_of_week: Tanzania Creator Economy Fund (TZS 2B, Ministry of ICT) launches in July 2026 — this week. Timamu must file legal registration in Tanzania immediately to be eligible for the September 30 expression-of-interest deadline. This is the largest domestic funding opportunity available and aligns perfectly with Vision 2050 disability inclusion mandates. Registration is the gating factor.

hot_list.why_now: September 1 deadline is 40 days away. Adobe Foundation prioritizes disability + creator economy. $50K grant is sized for early-stage orgs. Rolling deadline means early submission increases approval odds. First anchor funder for Timamu is strategically valuable.
hot_list.recommended_action: Submit concept note by August 15 (16 days early buffer). Emphasize: assistive technology + digital creative tools for PWDs in Tanzania. Frame as pilot for Phase 1. Use Adobe Creative Cloud accessibility features as evidence.

hot_list.why_now: October 1 deadline (70 days). Highest alignment score (93) + rolling applications. Microsoft AI for Accessibility is most accessible Tier 1 funder. Legal registration requirement likely cleared by mid-August; applies immediately after.
hot_list.recommended_action: Prepare LOI targeting $150K for assistive technology development + AI tools for PWDs (Phase 1 focus). Emphasize: AI accessibility gap in East Africa, Timamu's founder-led approach, partnership potential with Microsoft's global network.

hot_list.why_now: Fund activates NOW (July 2026). Expression-of-interest deadline September 30 (69 days). Timamu is uniquely positioned as disability-inclusive capability partner to government infrastructure play. TZS 2B (~USD 85M) is transformational scale.
hot_list.recommended_action: Submit EOI by September 10. Position: government funds creator platform infrastructure, Timamu delivers disability-inclusive curriculum + accessibility standards. Name Ministry of ICT contact. Reference Tanzania Vision 2050 disability mainstreaming commitment.

hot_list.why_now: Ford's April 2025 $15M disability rights commitment is still being deployed (12 months in). Detected priority shift towards technology + disability rights. No hard deadline yet, but 35% probability suggests targeted outreach this quarter could unlock Phase 2 (gig economy + AI) positioning.
hot_list.recommended_action: Prepare briefing document on Timamu's Phase 2 roadmap (gig economy + AI tools for PWDs). Research Ford's disability rights grantee cohort. Pitch: Ford's work reimagined story lab + Timamu's creator economy pathway = economic inclusion at scale.

hot_list.why_now: 88 alignment score. 2025-2026 Google AI for Accessibility commitments expanding. No tight deadline detected but high-tier strategic funder. Early relationship-building now pays off in Q4 2026 / Q1 2027 cycles.
hot_list.recommended_action: Research Google.org Africa accessibility grantee map. Identify 2-3 existing grantees doing complementary work (e.g., accessibility training, assistive tech). Request informational call with Google.org program officer to map collaboration potential before formal LOI in Q4.

hot_list.why_now: 87 alignment (disability-inclusive development + assistive tech + East Africa focus). No detected priority shift, but CBM is Tier 1 disability specialist with active Tanzania presence. No immediate deadline suggests relationship-building window.
hot_list.recommended_action: Request 30-minute call with CBM Tanzania country director. Present Timamu founder story + 9-10M PWD market opportunity. Explore: co-funding possibility, referral to other disability-focused funders, potential partnership on Phase 2 economic inclusion pilot.

hot_list.why_now: 84 alignment. Disability inclusion + economic empowerment for PWDs + East Africa. No deadline pressure but strong strategic fit. Complementary to CBM outreach — Humanity & Inclusion often co-funds disability initiatives.
hot_list.recommended_action: After CBM call, request intro to Humanity & Inclusion Tanzania programme manager. Position: Timamu as partner for economic inclusion pillar of disability-inclusive development work. Explore funding size ($100-250K range).

new_rfps.title: Tanzania Creator Economy Fund — Disability-Inclusive Programme Partnership
new_rfps.why_it_fits: TZS 2B fund launches July 2026 (this week). Timamu's disability-inclusive creator economy model directly addresses Vision 2050 targets. Government seeks capability partners. This is the most aligned domestic funding vehicle available. Legal registration in Tanzania is gating requirement.

priority_shifts.what_changed: April 2025: $15M new disability rights commitment — first in Ford's history. 25% disability inclusion across all grantmaking. Detected shift towards technology + disability rights (evidenced by Work Reimagined StoryLab partnership with Africa No Filter).
priority_shifts.action: Reposition Timamu pitch: from 'assistive tech for PWDs' to 'disability-centered creator economy = structural economic change.' Ford's theory of change favors systems-level interventions. Emphasize: Timamu disrupts creator economy gatekeeping for PWDs.

priority_shifts.what_changed: July 2026 (this week): TZS 2B creator economy fund activates. Vision 2050 implementation begins. Disability inclusion now mainstreamed into government funding strategy (CRPD commitments).
priority_shifts.action: Register Timamu Foundation in Tanzania within 14 days. Conduct pre-submission stakeholder mapping with Ministry of ICT (Digital Economy Directorate). Submit EOI by September 10.

out_of_industry.why_it_fits: Young Africa Works programme (2025-2026) explicitly expanding digital livelihoods focus. Emerging disability inclusion priority. Mastercard's creator economy interest + youth empowerment mandate = Timamu's exact niche. 80 alignment score suggests untapped opportunity.
out_of_industry.action: Research Mastercard Foundation's Young Africa Works grantee cohort in Tanzania. Identify 1-2 aligned orgs. Request warm introduction. Position: Timamu as disability-inclusive capability partner for Young Africa Works expansion in East Africa.

timamu.positioning_advice: Michael should publish or speak on: (1) **Disability-centered creator economy as economic inclusion model** — op-ed in African tech media (TechCrunch Africa, etc.) positioning Timamu as solving 9-10M PWD employment gap in Tanzania. Angle: Vision 2050 + gig economy + AI. Publish by August 15 to warm Ford Foundation + Mastercard conversations. (2) **Case study: Assistive tech adoption barriers in African creator platforms** — 500-word LinkedIn article + webinar proposal to Google.org accessibility network. Publish by August 30 to support October Microsoft LOI. (3) **Tanzania Vision 2050 disability inclusion readiness** — brief comment piece for government stakeholder audience (Ministry of ICT newsletter, Tanzanian development blogs). Frame Timamu as implementation partner. Publish by August 20 to support Tanzania creator fund EOI positioning. All three position Michael as thought leader on disability + creator economy at East African scale — essential credibility signal for Tier 1 funders.

cross_programme_conflicts.note: Same funder appears in both LearnImpact and Timamu pools. Coordinate before approaching — never allow simultaneous asks to same contact.
cross_programme_conflicts.recommended_sequence: Decide priority entity first. LearnImpact asks first if active programme ongoing.

This is the largest domestic funding opportunity available and aligns perfectly with Vision 2050 disability inclusion mandates. Registration is the gating factor.
